title: Advanced TypeScript: Leveraging Generics for Reusable Code

questions.sentence: As you transition to advanced TypeScript development, 'Generics' become an indispensable tool for creating flexible and reusable components and functions.
Generics allow you to define a component that can work with a variety of types while still maintaining 'type safety'.

For example, instead of writing separate functions to handle an array of strings and an array of numbers, you can use a generic type parameter—often represented as <T>—to handle any type provided at runtime.
Beyond simple generics, mastering 'Mapped Types' and 'Conditional Types' allows for highly sophisticated type transformations, such as creating a read-only version of an existing interface.
Utilizing these advanced features leads to a much more robust developer experience, as it provides precise IDE autocompletion and catches potential errors before the code is even run.
